Porsche Looks to Reduce Complexity by Cutting Models
articles.summary: Porsche AG confirmed its profit outlook for the year as it pushes to become leaner to revive margins battered by US tariffs and weak demand in China. David Welch has more on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Greenspan’s Legacy, From Irrational Exuberance to 2008 Crisis
articles.summary:
Tributes to former Federal Reserve Chairman Alan Greenspan poured in after the news of his death early Monday, many from former colleagues and market players who had a front-row seat during his 18-year tenure atop the US central bank.
Bill Dudley, Bloomberg Opinion Columnist and former NY Federal Reserve President, discusses the passing of Greenspan and how his role has influenced the Federal Reserve beyond his time there. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Caterpillar Inc Stock (CAT) Moved Down by 3.61% on Jun 23: Facts Behind the Movement
articles.summary: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) shares experienced a 3.61% dip on June 23, 2026, driven by broader market sell-offs, valuation concerns after a significant AI-driven rally, and investor profit-taking. Despite its recent surge to all-time highs due to its role in AI infrastructure through its Power and Energy division, the stock faces pressures from high valuation multiples, predicted tariff impacts of over $2 billion for fiscal year 2026, and notable insider stock sales. Technical indicators suggest close monitoring for investors.

articles.title: Jim Cramer Says “This Is a Terrific Level to Buy American Express”
articles.summary: Jim Cramer advised viewers that American Express (NYSE: AXP) is at a "terrific level to buy," suggesting a 50% position given its current 9% dip for the year. He linked the potential for an oil glut, cooled inflation, and lower interest rates due to Iran peace negotiations as factors that could benefit AXP and other credit card companies. Cramer highlighted CEO Steve Squeri's good work and expects consumer sentiment to improve as gasoline prices fall.

articles.title: Amazon Prime Day expected to drive record $26B in online spending
articles.summary: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is projected to generate a record $26 billion in online spending across all retailers, a 9% increase from the previous year. Despite ongoing inflation, shoppers are seeking discounts, and while competitors like Walmart and Target are offering promotions, Amazon is expected to capture 60% of the sales. This indicates sustained promotional intensity from retailers to attract budget-conscious consumers.

articles.title: Vintage photos of Beirut when it was known as the 'Paris of the Middle East'
articles.summary: Beirut, Lebanon's capital, was vibrant from the 1950s to the' 70s before a civil war ended what has been dubbed its "Golden Age."

articles.title: ECB’s Vujcic Sees Inflation Remaining Higher for Longer
articles.summary: The European Central Bank’s decision to raise interest rates was “robust” considering inflation will stay higher for longer, according to Vice President Boris Vujcic.

articles.title: TD (TD) launches callable barrier notes paying ~9.05% contingent coupon
articles.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank (TD) has launched Callable Contingent Interest Barrier Notes linked to the Nasdaq-100, Russell 2000, and S&P 500 indices, offering an annual contingent interest rate of approximately 9.05%. These notes mature on June 23, 2028, and pay monthly interest if each underlying index is at or above 75% of its initial value, but investors risk losing principal if any index falls below 70% of its initial value at maturity. TD can call the notes monthly starting from the sixth payment date, which introduces reinvestment risk for investors.

articles.title: Jim Cramer Says Darden “Almost Always Surprises to the Upside”
articles.summary: Jim Cramer expressed optimism about Darden Restaurants, Inc. (NYSE: DRI) ahead of its quarterly report, noting that the company "almost always surprises to the upside." He linked his positive outlook to potential oil glut from Iran peace talks leading to cooling inflation and lower interest rates, which could benefit the restaurant industry through reduced gas prices. Cramer also highlighted Darden, owner of Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse, as a retail winner, emphasizing its consistent performance.

articles.title: Taylor Says BOE Must be Ready to Cut Rates in Benign Scenario
articles.summary: Bank of England official Alan Taylor said policymakers need to be ready to act with interest-rate cuts should a benign scenario on inflation play out.

articles.title: Market could be on cusp of trend reversal; business cycle funds suitable for capturing sector opportunities, say experts
articles.summary: As the Indian stock market hints at a trend reversal, experts advocate for business cycle funds that promise diversified investments across shifting sectors. With strong recent performances, these funds allow investors to seize new opportunities within an evolving economy.

articles.title: US presidents' approval ratings when they left office, ranked

articles.title: Two Turkey Central Bank Chief Advisers Leave Their Roles

articles.title: US stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 futures tumble up to 3% as tech selloff deepens; Micron slides 7%
articles.summary: US stock futures indicated a steep decline on June 23, amid a tech sell-off led by SK Hynix's production cuts. The Nasdaq fell over 1.3%, affecting global markets. Concerns over AI spending and potential interest rate hikes further weighed on investor sentiment.

articles.title: Brazil Central Bank Flags Price Woes as Rate Cuts Near Pause
articles.summary: Brazil’s central bank said adjustments to its easing cycle will be made based on new information while also warning inflation risks are skewed to the upside, together boosting odds of a pause to interest rate cuts.

articles.title: Hungary Starts Rate-Cut Cycle as Strong Forint Tames Inflation
articles.summary: Hungary’s central bank cut one of the highest key interest rates in the European Union and said it had room for further monetary easing after a currency rally significantly improved the inflation outlook.

articles.title: From peace talks to Pennsylvania: Trump visiting Mack Truck facility
articles.summary: President Donald Trump is going to a Mack Truck facility in a battleground district in swing state Pennsylvania, casting attention on the U.S. economy in his first major public event beyond the capital since he signed the Iran war agreement

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc shares drop 5% amid slump in global silver prices

articles.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Jose Luis Escrivá said high oil prices are feeding through to other parts of the economy.

articles.summary: European Central Bank officials are facing the risk that inflation will hover above their goal “for quite some time,” according to Chief Economist Philip Lane.

articles.title: UK Economy Shrinks for Second Straight Month, PMI Shows
articles.summary: The UK economy shrank for a second consecutive month in June, according to a prominent private sector survey that showed firms cutting more jobs.

articles.title: Singapore’s Core Inflation Steady at 1.4% Despite Oil Crunch
articles.summary: Singapore’s core inflation rate was unchanged last month, even as the Middle East conflict had pushed up global prices of energy and fertilizer.

articles.title: Where to Invest 10 Lakh Rupees in India Confronting Inflation and Iran War Risks
articles.summary: Indian markets are showing signs of recovery, but volatile oil prices, a delayed monsoon, and uneven stock valuations could eat into those gains. Four financial experts point to investment opportunities in an otherwise nervous market.

articles.title: Hungary Likely to Cut Rates as Forint Gains and Inflation Slows
articles.summary: Hungary’s central bank is likely to lower borrowing costs for the first time in months as slowing inflation and a stronger currency give policymakers room to diverge from global peers starting monetary tightening.

articles.title: Rupee opens 1 paise lower at 94.69 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened weaker at 94.69 against the US dollar, down 1 paise. Market focus has shifted from oil prices to expectations of a US Federal Reserve rate hike in September, impacting Asian currencies including the rupee.

articles.title: India’s Inflation Is Anchored Despite May Pickup, RBI Says
articles.summary: Indian inflation remains anchored below the central bank’s target despite the recent energy shock, as pass-through to domestic prices has been limited, the Reserve Bank said in a monthly bulletin.
